# Nelly the Elephant MCP

> An elephant never forgets — and neither should your AI. Search and resume past Claude Code conversations via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## What is Nelly?

Nelly is an MCP server that searches your past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files stored in ~/.claude/projects/) and helps you find and resume old conversations. It searches across ALL projects at once, regardless of which project you're currently working in.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g nelly-elephant-mcp
```

Add to ~/.claude/mcp_settings.json:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "nelly": {
      "command": "nelly-elephant-mcp",
      "args": []
    }
  }
}
```

## Tools

- **nelly_instructions**: Returns usage guide, platform info, sessions directory status, and diagnostics
- **nelly_search**: Keyword search across all past sessions. Supports max_results (1-100), days_back (1-3650), and project filter. Returns date, project, match count, preview snippets, and resume command
- **nelly_context**: Expanded context from a specific session — shows surrounding lines around matches. Takes session_id, query, and context_lines (1-50)
- **nelly_recent**: Lists most recent sessions by modification time. Supports count (1-100) and project filter

## Trunk Modes

Nelly has two personality modes:

- **Long Trunk**: A conversational, character-driven mode where Nelly is a friendly elephant who guides you through the search with personality
- **Short Trunk**: Just results. Date, project, snippet, resume command. No narration.

Say "Nelly long trunk" or "Nelly short trunk" to switch anytime.

## Privacy and Security

- Zero network calls. Zero telemetry. Zero data collection.
- Reads only local JSONL files from ~/.claude/projects/
- Uses stdio transport only (no HTTP server)
- One runtime dependency: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
- Path traversal protection and symlink rejection built in
- MIT licensed, full source available

## Links

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/SpicyRiceCakes/nelly-elephant-mcp)
- [npm Package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/nelly-elephant-mcp)
- [Documentation Site](https://spicyricecakes.com/kitchen/nelly-the-elephant)

## For Optimization Freaks

"Should I `/clear` or start a new session?"

Here's the thing: Nelly searches the raw session files on disk. `/clear` resets Claude's memory but the session file keeps growing — every message before and after every `/clear` stays searchable. A new session creates a new file.

So:
- **`/clear` for same-topic continuation** — designing a feature, then building it, then debugging it? Same session. Nelly finds it as one result.
- **New session for new topics** — switching from your app to your website? New session. Nelly can search each one independently.
- **Multiple sessions on the same topic?** Totally fine. Nelly searches across all of them.

You can even use Nelly to find an old session, `--continue` into it, `/clear`, and keep working — the new work appends to the same file, all searchable.

But honestly? Don't overthink it. Use `/clear` when it feels right, start fresh when it feels right. Nelly handles the mess. That's literally the point.

Don't worry. Nelly's here.
